GN-1. Plaintiff (name): LAWRENCE LESS
alleges that defendant (name): “HTXS* TINGS COLLEGE OF THE LA WrCITY 2 COUNTTOF Sie F 4 eth het
FRANCHISEE: and CONVERSION MANAGEMENT ASSOCIATION, LLC: and
[7] Does 1 to. 25
was the legal (proximate) cause of damages to plaintiff. By the following acts or omissions to act, defendant
negligently caused the damage to plaintiff
on (date): or about 12/2/2014
at (place): or near the intersection of Leavenworth St. and McAllister St. in San F rancisco, CA
(description of reasons for liability): a
On or about December 2. 2014 Plaintiff Lawrence Less was walking on Leavenworth Street near
the corner of McAllister. As Plaintiff approached McAllister, he encountered a metal plate
embedded in the sidewalk that caused him to slip and fall. The metal plate was slick and occupied
a substantial portion of the sidewalk. The plate constituted a dangerous condition of public
property. Defendants, and-eachof them. identified in this cause of action only failed to maintain
or repair the metal plate. Further, these Defendants failed to warn of the dangerous condition or
guard against pedestrians encountering the plate.
As a direct and proximate result of the fall. Plaintiff sustained personal injuries including but not
limited to a serious elbow injury and a concussion among other injuries. Plaintiff has required
medical treatment including radiological studies. Plaintiff seeks compensation for past and future
medical damages, past and future wage loss, and past and future non-economic damages,
including but not limited to pain and suffering, loss of enjoyment of life. inconvenience, and
anxiety, in an amount to be determined at the time of trial.
Plaintiff is informed and believes and on the basis of said information and belief alleges that the
location where Plaintiff fell was owned and/or controlled by these Defendants. The area and the
metal plate that caused Plaintiff to fall was owned, maintained and controlled by these
Defendants.
Continued on Attachment A.

Prem.L-1. Plaintiff (name): LAWRENCE LESS
alleges the acts of defendants were the legal (proximate) cause of damages to plaintiff.
On (date): or about 12/2/2014 plaintiff was injured on the following premises in the following
fashion (description of premises and circumstances of injury):
While Plaintiff walked south on Leavenworth St. near McAllister St., Plaintiff encountered a
metal plate in the sidewalk that caused him to fall. The metal plate was slick and occupied a
substantial portion of the sidewalk. The plate constituted a dangerous condition of public
property, as it did not have any anti-slip surface or guard around it. Defendants failed to maintain
or repair the metal plate. Further, the Defendants failed to warn of the dangerous condition or
guard against pedestrians encountering the plate. Plaintiff was walking to perform work at the law
library on Market at the time of the incident.
Prem.L-2: CH) Count One—Negligence The defendants who negligently owned, maintained, Managed and
